description:
Check out how to run autonomous AI agents safely on your Mac by creating an isolated "Inception-style" sandbox using Colima, Incus, and Claude Code. This tutorial walks you through solving common security risks and networking hurdles to build a collaborative multi-agent system that can develop apps and analyze files without ever touching your host machine. Whether you're building software or inspecting suspicious files, this setup provides the ultimate balance of AI power and impenetrable container security.

🔗 Relevant Links
Code On Incus: https://github.com/mensfeld/code-on-incus
Incus: https://linuxcontainers.org/incus/
Colima: https://github.com/abiosoft/colima

❤️ More about us
Radically better observability stack: https://betterstack.com/
Written tutorials: https://betterstack.com/community/
Example projects: https://github.com/BetterStackHQ

📌 Chapters:
00:00 Introduction: The AI Security Problem
01:01 What is Incus?
01:21 How Code on Incus Works
01:46 Persistent State & UID Mapping
02:18 Setup: Running Incus on Mac
03:01 Installing & Configuring Incus
03:45 Network & Firewall Fixes
04:37 Building the 'coi' Tool
05:13 Demo 1: Multi-Agent Collaboration
06:45 Demo 2: Secure Malware Analysis
08:43 Final Thoughts & Conclusion
fulltitle: This Sandbox Tool Makes Claude Code Unstoppable (Code On Incus)
